content: The goal is to create a comprehensive `memory-bank/reflection/reflection-[feature_id].md` document.
The reflection document must include the Feature Name & ID and the Date of Reflection.
The reflection document must include a brief summary of the feature, answering what was built.
Assess and document how well the final feature met the initial requirements and note any deviations from the original scope and their reasons.
The reflection document must provide an overall assessment of the feature's success.
Review the effectiveness of the guidance from `Level3/planning-comprehensive.mdc` and the accuracy of the initial plan in `tasks.md`.
Review the effectiveness of design decisions and how well they translated into implementation, noting any friction points.
Assess if the style guide (`memory-bank/style-guide.md`) was clear and sufficient for UI aspects.
Document the major successes, biggest challenges, and any unexpected technical difficulties during the implementation phase.
Assess adherence to the style guide and coding standards during implementation.
Review the effectiveness of the testing strategy and whether it uncovered significant issues early enough.
Identify what could improve the testing process for similar features.
Highlight 3-5 key positives and identify 3-5 areas for improvement across all phases of the feature development.
Identify and document key lessons learned regarding technical aspects, development process, and work estimation.
Propose specific, actionable improvements for future L3 feature development.
The reflection should focus on Feature Scope Management to assess if the scope was well-defined and managed.
The reflection should focus on Integration Complexity to understand challenges or successes in integrating the feature.
The reflection should focus on Design-to-Implementation Fidelity to assess how closely the final product matched the designs.
The reflection should focus on understanding the Cross-Component Impact of the feature.
